description: Instruction set for validating YouTube Mirror campaign context lists across three quality dimensions

severity_definitions.error: Counts toward error threshold. Only 3 rules carry this severity — all in CHECK 2 (alignment issues).
severity_definitions.warning: Always highlighted in output. NEVER counted as error. NEVER affects training label. Informs human reviewer only.
severity_definitions.recommendation: Always shown. Informational only. CHECK 3 always produces recommendations, never errors.
severity_definitions.info: Lightweight awareness flag. No action required.

training_label_logic.description: Determines how a validated context list is stored in Supabase training database
training_label_logic.important_note: Warnings are NEVER counted as errors. A list with 50 warnings and 0 errors is still PASS_WITH_WARNINGS and stored as POSITIVE_EXAMPLE. This is intentional — context lists have large signal volumes and warning counts will naturally be high.

rules.note: Warnings highlighted so model learns what to watch for, but list is structurally sound
One specific alignment mistake with clear reasoning — valuable negative training example
Too many fundamental issues — too noisy to be useful for training

checks.description: Signal hygiene checks. NO hard errors in this check. All rules are warnings or info — always highlighted but never block storage or affect training label.
checks.note: There is NO limit on number of Tactics or Sub-Tactics. These are not validated.

rules.description: Signals longer than 3 words should always be flagged. Ideal signal length is 2-3 words.
rules.flag_message: Signal '{signal}' under Sub-Tactic '{sub_tactic}' is longer than 3 words. Signals should be 2-3 words for precise YouTube contextual matching.

rules.description: Identical signals appearing more than once across the entire context list. Always highlighted — never counted as error.
rules.validation: No two signals in the entire list are identical (case-insensitive)
rules.flag_message: Duplicate signal found: '{signal}' appears more than once. Remove all but one instance.

rules.description: For celebrities, brands, products, channels, teams — only the canonical full name should appear. Short forms, nicknames and name variations are all treated as duplicates of the canonical name.
rules.validation: For each proper noun entity, only one canonical signal referencing it should exist
rules.flag_message: Proper noun duplicate detected. Keep only the canonical full name: '{canonical_signal}'. Remove variations: {duplicates}.

rules.description: Signals must not use standalone abbreviations with multiple possible meanings (e.g. CPA, RDJ, SRK, NRT, OTC)
rules.validation: Signal does not contain standalone abbreviations
rules.flag_message: Signal '{signal}' uses an abbreviation. Use the full term to avoid ambiguity.

rules.description: Single standalone generic words reduce targeting precision
rules.validation: Signal is not a standalone generic word with no targeting specificity
rules.flag_message: Signal '{signal}' may be too generic. Consider a more specific 2-3 word phrase.

rules.description: When no sub-categorisation is needed under a Tactic, Sub-Tactic should be labeled 'All'
rules.validation: If a Tactic has only one Sub-Tactic and it is not labeled 'All', flag for review
rules.flag_message: Tactic '{tactic_name}' has a single Sub-Tactic not labeled 'All'. If no sub-categorisation is needed, rename to 'All'.

rules.description: Exclusion keywords should be contextual phrases, not single broad words
rules.validation: Exclusion keywords should not be single standalone words
rules.flag_message: Exclusion keyword '{keyword}' is too broad. Use a contextual phrase to avoid over-blocking.

checks.description: THE ONLY CHECK THAT PRODUCES ERRORS. Three rules, all severity: error. Error count here directly determines training label.

rules.description: Each Tactic must have a clear and explainable connection to the campaign brief, brand, vertical, geo and target audience. If no logical rationale can be constructed — it is an error.
rules.validation: For each Tactic: can a clear rationale be constructed linking it to the brand, vertical, geo and target audience?
rules.flag_message: Tactic '{tactic_name}' does not appear relevant to the campaign brief. Brief: '{brief_summary}'. Please justify or remove.

rules.description: Each Sub-Tactic must be a logical sub-category of its parent Tactic — not a standalone or misplaced theme.
rules.validation: Sub-Tactic is semantically a subcategory of its parent Tactic
rules.flag_message: Sub-Tactic '{sub_tactic_name}' is not a logical sub-category of Tactic '{tactic_name}'. Review placement.

rules.description: Each Signal must be a specific keyword that would identify YouTube content relevant to its parent Sub-Tactic.
rules.validation: Each Signal identifies YouTube content that fits its parent Sub-Tactic context
rules.flag_message: Signal '{signal}' does not appear relevant to Sub-Tactic '{sub_tactic_name}'. Review or relocate.

checks.check_name: Thematic Alignment for Sensitive & Niche Campaigns
checks.description: ALWAYS produces recommendations — never errors. Checks if campaign is sensitive/niche and suggests broader thematic Tactics to improve delivery scale. Model uses this to learn what broader categories to add for such campaigns.

trigger_conditions.description: Activates when ANY of the following are true
conditions: vertical is in the sensitive_verticals list
geo targeting is DMA-level or city/district level
age_group span is less than 15 years
budget is described as high or brief implies large-scale delivery
brief mentions a specific medicine, financial product, regulated substance, or niche product

rules.flag_message: Vertical '{vertical}' is sensitive/regulated. Thematic alignment recommendations follow.
Niche targeting detected ({targeting_detail}). Broader Tactics recommended for delivery scale.

rules.description: Recommend up to 3 Tactics/Sub-Tactics from playbook not already in the context list. Always a recommendation — never an error.
rules.instruction: Look up vertical in playbook_data. Find subcategories NOT already in existing Tactics. Recommend up to 3 most relevant additions for this specific campaign audience.
rules.flag_message: Suggest adding broader themes for scale: {recommended_tactics}. Based on vertical playbook for '{vertical}'.

output_schema.description: Structured output stored in Supabase after each validation run

triggered_rules.reasoning: Specific explanation tied to this campaign — not a generic message. Always explain WHY referencing the actual brand, brief, audience.

fields.full_validation_report: Complete JSON stored as-is for model training reference
